adds.rationale: Welltower's C$4.6B acquisition of 38 Amica ultra-luxury senior housing communities (announced March 2, 2025) and strong Q4 2024 operating results (normalized FFO +17.7% YoY, senior housing same-store NOI +23.9%) deliver a concrete commercial milestone that gives the demographics wave its first watchlist representation, while also adding a defensive non-tech position at a time when DeepSeek and tariff fears are punishing AI-heavy tech names.

news_evidence.summary: Welltower announced acquisition of 38 ultra-luxury Amica Senior Lifestyles communities from Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan for C$4.6B, forming a long-term senior housing growth partnership — the single largest senior housing transaction in Welltower's history

Welltower Q4 2024 earnings: quarterly revenue $2.25B (beat analyst estimate of $2.12B), normalized FFO up 17.7% YoY to $1.13/share, Seniors Housing Operating same-store NOI +23.9%; net debt/EBITDA improved to 3.49x from 5.03x a year earlier

Health care REITs' defensive attributes kept the sector in favor heading into 2025; aging-population secular demand insulates the sector from macro uncertainty — outperforming in the same Q1 environment where AI-heavy tech names sold off on DeepSeek and tariff fears

removes.rationale: Quantum computing stocks hit peak-enthusiasm multiples after Google's Willow chip announcement (December 2024), leaving IONQ at roughly 150-200x trailing revenue entering Q1 2025; Amazon's Ocelot quantum chip (February 27, 2025) then showed hyperscalers building quantum capability in-house, creating a structural competitive threat to pure-play vendors — and GOOGL already in the watchlist provides quantum-theme exposure via Google's own Willow research program.

news_evidence.summary: Amazon Web Services announced Ocelot, its first quantum computing chip, on February 27, 2025 — built around cat-qubit architecture at Caltech, reducing error-correction resource requirements by up to 90%; a direct signal that hyperscalers are internalizing quantum R&D rather than procuring from pure-play vendors

IonQ Q4 2024 results: full-year 2024 revenue $43.1M (+95% YoY), Q4 revenue $11.7M, bookings $95.6M; 2025 guidance $75-95M — strong growth but a market cap implied by the post-Willow surge priced in revenues many years out, producing a P/S ratio the sell-side itself called 'stratospheric' and 'completely overvalued'

Seeking Alpha analysis: 'IonQ: Is Falling Behind Its Competitors While Being Completely Overvalued' — stock trading at P/S ratios exceeding 100x vs tech-industry average of ~4x, with hyperscalers (Google Willow, now AWS Ocelot) accelerating in-house quantum programs that crowd out third-party pure plays

rationale_overall: One targeted swap: IONQ exits and WELL enters, keeping the total at the eight-ticker cap. Every other named wave in the profile retains coverage — AI through GOOGL, MSFT, and NVDA; robotics through BOTZ; nuclear through NUKZ; rockets/spacecraft through RKLB; defense/geopolitical through ITA — while the demographics wave, the one declared profile wave with zero representation, gets its first slot via Welltower. The IONQ remove is not a thesis rejection of quantum computing; it is an application of the profile's 'trim before the crest' rule: the post-Willow surge in December 2024 left IONQ at 150-200x revenue just as AWS Ocelot (February 27, 2025) confirmed hyperscalers are building quantum in-house, and GOOGL already provides quantum-wave exposure through Google's own Willow program. WELL enters on the strength of Welltower's record C$4.6B Amica acquisition (March 2, 2025) and a Q4 2024 earnings beat, and also brings meaningful diversification benefit — a defensive, income-generating non-tech REIT at a moment when the DeepSeek shock and early tariff anxiety are weighing on the AI-heavy positions.
